title: Smart building positioning system
caption: A high-level view of the hub: wireless signals, indoor floor geometry, smartphones, and route traces become one positioning service for smart buildings and academic research visualization.

title: Context descriptor c(t)
caption: This visual groups motion state, carrying mode, turns, stops, vertical transitions, indoor-outdoor gates, and data-quality events into a compact context layer for adaptive positioning.

title: Phone pose and trajectory behavior
caption: The same pedestrian route can look different under swinging, handheld, calling, mixed, and pocket use. The figure explains why pose-aware correction is central to smartphone PDR.

title: Reliability-governed radio-map write-back
caption: Candidate fingerprints pass through spatial, feature, denoising, confidence, and structural gates before they are allowed to update the radio map.

title: Crowd-powered IPS deployment realities
caption: Scalable indoor positioning must handle battery limits, privacy permissions, missing scans, carrying mode changes, cross-building differences, and real user behavior.

title: Multisensor session log stream
caption: A mobile session y(t) can aggregate Wi-Fi, IMU, barometer, magnetometer, GNSS, and application events, turning raw sensing into a time-ordered spatial evidence stream.

title: Skeleton, assembly, and refinement
caption: Floor-local route skeletons are assembled into a building-local 3D structure and refined using access-point anchors, gate constraints, and global geometric adjustment.

title: Principles for scaling IPS
caption: The conceptual goal is a generic, self-healing, user-centered IPS that learns from ordinary user data while reducing dependency on skilled surveyors and device-specific calibration.
